# CLI Catalog — `loops` / `sl`

Exhaustive, source-derived reference: every verb and command with its status and verbatim `--help`. Generated from `loops.cli.registry` and each command's argparse parser — it cannot drift from the installed surface.

For the dispatch model, curated overview, and the Python library API, see [api-reference.md](api-reference.md). For worked examples and syntax depth, see [CLI-CHEATSHEET.md](CLI-CHEATSHEET.md).

## Verbs (`registry.VERBS`)

Primary vertex operations: `loops <verb> [vertex] [args]`.

### `loops read`

```text
usage: loops read [-h] [--kind KIND] [--key KEY] [--lens LENS] [--facts]
                  [--diff] [-q] [-v] [--plain] [--json] [--static] [--live]
                  [-i] [--max-chars MAX_CHARS] [--max-lines MAX_LINES]
                  [vertex_or_entity] [entity]

positional arguments:
  vertex_or_entity      Vertex name, path, or kind/key entity
  entity                Entity filter (kind/key)

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --kind KIND           Filter by fact kind
  --key KEY             Filter by fold key (prefix scan with trailing /)
  --lens LENS           Lens name for rendering
  --facts               Show raw fact stream instead of folded state
  --diff                Show only facts not yet reflected in the fold
  -q, --quiet           Minimal output (counts only)
  -v, --verbose         Increase verbosity (-v detailed, -vv full)
  --plain               Plain text output (no color)
  --json                JSON output
  --static              Static render (no live updates)
  --live                Live updating display
  -i, --interactive     Interactive TUI explorer
  --max-chars MAX_CHARS
                        Truncate output at N characters
  --max-lines MAX_LINES
                        Truncate output at N lines
```

### `loops emit`

```text
usage: loops emit [-h] [--observer OBSERVER] [--dry-run] [--strict] [-q]
                  [--stdin FIELD] [--file FIELD=PATH]
                  [vertex] kind [parts ...]

positional arguments:
  vertex               Vertex name or .vertex path (auto-resolves local
                       vertex)
  kind                 Fact kind
  parts                KEY=VALUE pairs and optional trailing message text

options:
  -h, --help           show this help message and exit
  --observer OBSERVER  Observer string (defaults to .vertex declaration /
                       $LOOPS_OBSERVER)
  --dry-run            Print the fact JSON without storing
  --strict             Refuse on validation failures (unknown kind, missing
                       fold-key, unresolved ref). Overridden by vertex 'strict
                       true' declaration.
  -q, --quiet          Suppress the 'stored:' success receipt (WARN/ERROR
                       still print).
  --stdin FIELD        Read sys.stdin into the named payload field (e.g.
                       --stdin message). Sidesteps shell-quoting for natural-
                       voice prose. Errors on TTY. Single trailing newline
                       stripped.
  --file FIELD=PATH    Read file contents into the named payload field (e.g.
                       --file message=notes.md). May repeat for different
                       fields. Tilde expansion supported. Single trailing
                       newline stripped.
```

### `loops close`

```text
usage: loops close [-h] [--dry-run] [vertex] kind name [message]

positional arguments:
  vertex
  kind        Fact kind to close (e.g. thread, task)
  name        Name/key of the item to close
  message     Resolution summary

options:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit
  --dry-run   Show what would happen
```

### `loops sync`

```text
usage: loops sync [-h] [--force] [--var KEY=VALUE] [vertex]

positional arguments:
  vertex           Vertex name or path

options:
  -h, --help       show this help message and exit
  --force, -f      Run all sources unconditionally
  --var KEY=VALUE  Variable override
```

### `loops cite`

```text
usage: loops cite [-h] [--context CONTEXT] [-m MESSAGE] [--dry-run]
                  refs [refs ...]

positional arguments:
  refs                  kind/key refs or bare ULIDs — the attention targets

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --context CONTEXT     Optional thread or task name to tag the citation
  -m, --message MESSAGE
                        Optional in-the-moment context for the citation
  --dry-run             Print the fact JSON without storing
```

### `loops store`

```text
usage: loops store [-h] [-i] [-q] [-v] [--json] [--plain] [file]

Inspect store contents.

positional arguments:
  file               Store .db or .vertex file, or vertex name

options:
  -h, --help         show this help message and exit
  -i, --interactive  Interactive TUI explorer
  -q, --quiet        Minimal output
  -v, --verbose      Detailed output
  --json             JSON output
  --plain            Plain text, no ANSI codes
```

## Commands (`registry.COMMANDS`)

Dev/setup actions that need no vertex resolution: `loops <command> [args]`.

### `loops test`

```text
usage: loops test [-h] [--input FILE] [--limit N] file

Test a .loop file — preview facts without persistence.

positional arguments:
  file              Loop file to test

options:
  -h, --help        show this help message and exit
  --input, -i FILE  Input file to feed through parse pipeline
  --limit, -n N     Max facts to show
```

### `loops compile`

```text
usage: loops compile [-h] file

Show compiled structure of a .loop or .vertex file.

positional arguments:
  file        Loop or vertex file to compile

options:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit
```

### `loops validate`

```text
usage: loops validate [-h] [-q] [-v] [--json] [--plain] [files ...]

Validate .loop or .vertex files.

positional arguments:
  files          Files to validate (default: all .loop/.vertex in cwd)

options:
  -h, --help     show this help message and exit
  -q, --quiet    Minimal output
  -v, --verbose  Detailed output
  --json         JSON output
  --plain        Plain text, no ANSI codes
```

### `loops init`

```text
usage: loops init [-h] [--template TEMPLATE] [name] [seed ...]

positional arguments:
  name                  Vertex name (e.g., 'project' or 'dev/project')
  seed                  key=value pairs to emit as config facts after creation

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --template, -t TEMPLATE
                        Source vertex name to use as template (defaults to
                        init name)
```

### `loops whoami`

```text
usage: loops whoami [-h]

Show resolved observer identity. No arguments.

options:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit
```

### `loops ls`

```text
usage: loops ls [-h] [vertex]

List all vertices, or declarations for one vertex.

Section narrowing (flag form, composable):
  --kind [NAME]         show KINDS section (or one named entry)
  --observer [NAME]     show OBSERVERS section (or one named entry)
  --combine [PATH]      show COMBINE section (or one named entry)
  --row [TEMPLATE]      show SOURCES section (or one template)

Positional alias (back-compat, single section, no narrowing):
  loops ls <vertex> kind|observer|combine|row

Mixing both forms is an error.

positional arguments:
  vertex      Vertex name (omit for root listing)

options:
  -h, --help  show this help message and exit
```

### `loops add`

```text
usage: loops add [-h] vertex [{kind,observer,combine,row}]

Add a declaration to a vertex file, or a row to a template population.

positional arguments:
  vertex                Vertex name
  {kind,observer,combine,row}
                        kind / observer / combine / row (default: row)

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
```

### `loops rm`

```text
usage: loops rm [-h] vertex [{kind,observer,combine,row}]

Remove a declaration from a vertex file, or a row from a template population.

positional arguments:
  vertex                Vertex name
  {kind,observer,combine,row}
                        kind / observer / combine / row (default: row)

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
```

### `loops export`

**RETIRED (Phase 3) — still registered, but returns an error**

## Vertex-first shorthand (`registry.VERTEX_OPS`)

Dispatchable as `loops <vertex> <op>` (a bare `loops <vertex>` is implicit `read`):

`add`, `cite`, `close`, `emit`, `export`, `ls`, `read`, `rm`, `store`, `sync`
